behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a local module handoff boundary (diff parser -> review-input builder). Unit could miss contract mismatch between these modules. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text is only about scoping logic, not real HTTP/OS/subprocess, and not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser + prompt/input-construction modules together on a fixture PR diff where unchanged lines include tempting defects; assert produced review payload contains only changed hunks/lines and excludes unchanged context beyond allowed minimal metadata.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is GitHub diff-position semantics over real API calls. In-process mocks commonly accept invalid/shifted positions that GitHub rejects or renders differently (MOCK-HIDE). Higher levels were rejected because correctness does not depend on model judgment; this is API/positioning behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed positions for several changed lines (additions/modifications near hunk edges), then fetch review comments from GitHub and verify each appears on the intended diff line/path in the PR UI/API.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment (detecting an actual defect). Stubbed outputs would always pass and hide model-quality failures. Workflow was rejected because behavior text requires one detection outcome, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a seeded PR/diff containing a known bug pattern; run real model-based analysis and assert at least one finding correctly targets the known defect (allowing non-determinism via repeated runs or acceptance threshold).

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state/ID consistency (inline-post results -> summary assembler) and can fail without any external infra. Unit is too low because bug appears at handoff between posting-result collection and summary construction. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is internal correspondence, not model-quality or real-OS/network dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Execute comment-posting adapter (stubbed API responses returning IDs) plus summary builder in one run context; assert each summary entry references an ID emitted in that same run and no foreign/stale IDs are present.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Failure is loss across pipeline stages (findings list -> posted-inline map -> summary payload), i.e., module interaction/state propagation. Unit may miss drop conditions introduced by stage coupling. Higher levels rejected because behavior does not require real API/model output to reproduce dropping.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration with multiple findings (including edge cases like duplicates/severity variants) through inline-post result aggregation and summary-payload generation; assert payload cardinality and IDs cover all inline findings exactly once per expected policy.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real pull request and live API sequence; real HTTP/API ordering/auth/rate-limit/validation failures are the target boundary. Agentic/Workflow rejected by concrete text 'fixed precomputed finding set' (no model decision dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end against a real test PR using fixed findings: fetch diff, post inline comments, then post summary; assert all API calls succeed, posted artifacts exist, and summary references posted comment IDs.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Execution path includes real model-generated findings, so stubs can hide failures tied to model output shape/content. System was considered but rejected as final because behavior text adds LLM-dependent analysis stage; minimum must cover model non-determinism. Workflow was considered and rejected because text specifies a single agentic stage ('analyze') followed by deterministic posting steps, not multiple agentic decision stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run full automation on a real test PR with real model inference enabled; assert pipeline completes unattended, produces non-empty valid findings, successfully posts inline comments, and publishes a consistent summary for that run.
